description: Select VLA as the job type in the fine-tuning wizard
steps: In the wizard Step 1, click the 'VLA' button (camera icon)
explanation: VLA = Vision-Language-Action. Fine-tunes robotics policies on multi-camera datasets. Requires Plus or Pro — on Basic the option is locked and the wizard reverts to LLM.
expected_outcome: VLA is selected; VLA model cards become visible

validation.rule: VLA button is highlighted in orange; VLA model cards (ACT, SmolVLA, Pi0.5, GR00T N1.5) are visible. No 'locked' overlay.

common_errors: VLA option locked / wizard reverts to LLM — you are on the Basic plan; upgrade to Plus or Pro first

description: Select a base VLA policy model

act.label: ACT
act.description: Predicts chunks of future actions from multi-camera images and robot state. Strong fit for fine manipulation and imitation learning.

smolvla.label: SmolVLA
smolvla.description: Compact ~450M VLA from HuggingFace. VLM backbone with flow-matching action head. Efficient training, edge-friendly.
smolvla.best_for: Efficient training on limited data; edge deployment

pi05.label: Pi0.5
pi05.description: Physical Intelligence VLA for open-world generalization. Uses lerobot/pi05_base checkpoint.

groot_n15.label: GR00T N1.5
groot_n15.description: NVIDIA humanoid VLA with vision-language input and flow-style action modeling. Strong language following.

validation.rule: One of the four VLA model cards is highlighted/selected

camera_feeds.description: Number of cameras in your dataset. MUST match the dataset AND must fit in the selected GPU's VRAM.

critical_warning: Camera count affects VRAM requirements per sample. Higher camera counts on smaller GPUs (A100 40GB) will fail at provisioning. Pi0.5 and GR00T N1.5 recommend A100 80GB — match your camera count to your GPU.

validation.rule: Parameters set; camera_feeds matches what your dataset actually contains

description: Connect a LeRobot-compatible dataset for VLA fine-tuning

dataset_repo_id.description: HuggingFace repo ID of a LeRobot-compatible dataset

camera_feeds.description: Must match the actual number of cameras in your dataset. Mismatch = job failure.

dataset_format.standard: LeRobot-compatible (episodes, frames, observations, actions layout per LeRobot spec)
dataset_format.metadata: meta/info.json must contain dataset_type, fps, features, and robot_type

example_datasets_from_solo.description: Tabletop object pick task with single/dual camera, short episodes
Liquid pour / serving sequence with fine manipulation

critical_warning: Camera count mismatch is the most common VLA job failure. Verify your dataset's actual camera count before setting this value.
expected_outcome: Dataset connected; camera count matches dataset

validation.rule: Dataset repo ID filled; camera count matches dataset metadata (check meta/info.json on HuggingFace)
